revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Arata-jinja (Iwade)]][[ja:荒田神社 (岩出市)]] {{Expand Japanese|荒田神社 (岩出市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Arata Shrine | native_name = 荒田神社 | image = Arata-jinja (Iwade) honden.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Arata Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.272631|135.308013}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Arata Shrine'''|荒田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kii Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11604948|ltq=Q11604948|qid=Q11604948|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kii Province}} ** Series ordinal: 3 ** P155: {{ill|Niutsuhime Shrine|lt=Niutsuhime Shrine|cs|Svatyně Niucuhime|en|Niutsuhime Shrine|eu|Niutsuhime santutegia|fa|معبد نیوتسوهیمه|fr|Niutsuhime-jinja|it|Santuario Niutsuhime|ja|丹生都比売神社|tr|Niutsuhime Tapınağı|zh|丹生都比賣神社|qid=Q705154|1=Niutsuhime Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q134888898|ltq=Q134888898|qid=Q134888898|1=Kaijin Shrine (Kinokawa)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == کیئی|fr|Province de Kii|gl|Provincia de Kii|id|Provinsi Kii|it|Kii (provincia)|ja|紀伊国|ko|기이국|lt|Kijo žemė|nl|Kii (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Kii|pt|Província de Kii|ru|Кии (провинция)|simple|Kii Province|sk|Kii (provincia)|sr|Провинција Киј|th|แคว้นคิอิ|tr|Kii (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Кії|vi|Tỉnh Kii|zh_min_nan|Kii-kok|zh|紀伊國|qid=Q864053|1=Kii Province}} * {{ill|Q1093809|ltq=Q1093809|qid=Q1093809|1=Naga District, Wakayama}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.272631|135.308013}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Arata-jinja (Iwade) honden.JPG == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183136 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917284|ltq=Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4170005002888 == Commons category (P373) == * Arata-jinja (Iwade) == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Engishiki seat|lt=Engishiki seat|qid=Q135018062|1=Engishiki seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '和歌山県岩出市大字森237番地', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '和歌山県岩出市森', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Arata-jinja (Iwade)}} [[JA:荒田神社 (岩出市)]] {{wikidata link|Q17191921}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Arata Shrine |Image = [[File:Arata-jinja (Iwade) honden.JPG|280px]]
Main hall (designated cultural property of Wakayama Prefecture) |Location = {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|qid=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwade City|Q579623|qid=Q579623|1=Iwade, Wakayama}} Ōaza Mori 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|21.94|N|135|18|28.79|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Arata Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto and 23 others |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|lt=Hiwadabuki|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |Other name = |Pilgrimage = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 5|Q2930|qid=Q2930|1=October 5}} |Rituals = New Year Suzu-tamari ritual |Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Arata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Arata-jinja (Iwade) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Arata Shrine''' (Arata-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mori, {{ill|lt=Iwade City|Q579623|qid=Q579623|1=Iwade, Wakayama}}, {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|qid=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines the following 24 deitiesThe list of enshrined deities is based on the explanatory sign within the shrine grounds.. * {{ill|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11620893|qid=Q11620893|lt=Tsurugine-no-Mikoto|1=Katsuragi no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|qid=Q13579836|lt=Ama-no-Somukitsu-Hime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|lt=Niutsuhime Shrine|Q705154|qid=Q705154|lt=Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Niutsuhime Shrine}}, {{ill|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|qid=Q10935105|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}, {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|qid=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōyashima-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shirahige Daimyōjin|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Harai-do no Ōkami|Q11588504|qid=Q11588504|lt=Haraido no Kyūhashira no Kami|1=Haraedo no Kami}} === History === === Overview === The ancient origins are unknown. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} lists “Arata Shrine, two seats” in {{ill|lt=Naga District (Wakayama Prefecture)|Q1093809|qid=Q1093809|lt=Naga District|1=Naga District, Wakayama}}, {{ill|lt=Kii Province|Q864053|qid=Q864053|1=Kii Province}}, placing it among the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. The {{ill|lt=Register of Deities of Kii Province|Q17210008|qid=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}} records it as a local deity with the rank “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade, Arata-no-Kami.” According to the {{ill|lt=Kii Zokufudoki|Q11604964|qid=Q11604964|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki}} (an Edo-period gazetteer of Kii Province), the enshrined deities were {{ill|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}, the ancestor of the Arata no Atae clan, and {{ill|lt=Katsuragi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11620893|qid=Q11620893|lt=Tsurugine-no-Mikoto|1=Katsuragi no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, and later—like {{ill|lt=Hirota Shrine|Q1466105|qid=Q1466105|1=Hirota Shrine (Osaka City)}} in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}—{{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|qid=Q13579836|lt=Ama-no-Somukitsu-Hime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} were added. Because this {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ama-no-Somukitsu-Hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} is considered the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aramitama|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, it is thought that Arata Shrine originated from {{ill|lt=Aragami|Q6455190|qid=Q6455190|lt=aragami worship|1=Kōjin}}“Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture,” entry for Arata Shrine.. The same source states that the shrine was destroyed by warfare during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|qid=Q6678|1=1573}}–{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|qid=Q6769|1=1592}}). In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period, under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade ({{ill|lt=Register of Deities of Kii Province|Q17210008|qid=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}) — recorded as “Arata-no-Kami.” === Shrine grounds === [[File:Arata-jinja (Iwade) keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Sacred grove and worship hall within the grounds}}]] The main hall was rebuilt in the first year of {{ill|lt=Kan’ei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} ({{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|qid=Q6821|1=1624}}). It is a sangensha {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a {{ill|lt=Hiwadabuki|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} roof. The entire structure is vermilion-lacquered and adorned with carvings said to have been donated by {{ill|lt=Heinai Masanobu|Q11482097|qid=Q11482097|lt=Heiuchi Masanobu|1=Heinouchi Masanobu}}. This main hall is designated a {{ill|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860|qid=Q2901860|lt=Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} of {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|qid=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}. === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Inari Shrine — enshrined deities: {{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}} * Niu Shrine * Yasaka Shrine === Festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival (January 1)Festival information is based on the explanatory leaflet within the shrine grounds. * Spring Festival (February 19) * Ohatsuho (June 30) * Gion Festival (July 7) * Summer Festival (August 19) * Tanomo Festival (August 31) * Autumn Festival (October 5) * Shichi-Go-San pilgrimage (early November) * Ohatsuho (November 24) * Ohitaki (late December) In addition, there is a special ritual called the New Year Suzu-tamari ritual[Arata Shrine] (Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association).. === Cultural properties === === Wakayama Prefecture–designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main hall (structure) — designated April 11, 1995. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine grounds explanatory sign * “{{ill|lt=Japan Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}}: Wakayama Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Naga District, Arata Shrine entry === External links === {{Commonscat|Arata-jinja (Iwade)}} * [Arata Shrine] — Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwade, Wakayama]] [[Category:岩出市の歴史]]
